title: Lab: Build and Measure an Informational Orbit
summary: Lab: Build and Measure an Informational Orbit retrieves prior skills in an original independent keyframe composition performance and preference build scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css transitions, css keyframes, css animation timing, css animation performance, css reduced motion, css animation debug in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent keyframe composition performance and preference build artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Ethical Product Launch motion and feedback work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the Ethical Product Launch motion and feedback work here. */

steps.title: 01 · timeline and rendering measurement predict 1 · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Build an orbiting service-status indicator for a transit display where the static label remains authoritative and motion only explains update cadence. Which conclusion is justified by a computed-style trace, animation-frame measurement, paused and interrupted states, and an equivalent reduced-motion status? Scenario: independent keyframe composition performance and preference build.
steps.why: Lab: Build and Measure an Informational Orbit makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent keyframe composition performance and preference build.

content.title: independent keyframe composition performance and preference build evidence
content.text: For Lab: Build and Measure an Informational Orbit, choose the css transitions claim that preserves the independent keyframe composition performance and preference build purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Lab: Build and Measure an Informational Orbit checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Lab: Build and Measure an Informational Orbit checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Lab: Build and Measure an Informational Orbit checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 1: transition all is harmless because browsers automatically choose only cheap useful properties. Treat the timeline and rendering measurement appearance as sufficient proof.

Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 1: name the exact state change and properties, keep both endpoints understandable, and verify interruption, keyboard, preference, and performance behavior. Apply that contract to timeline and rendering measurement and retain changed-case evidence.

Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 1: delay button activation until its hover transition finishes so users see the complete effect. Skip a computed-style trace, animation-frame measurement, paused and interrupted states, and an equivalent reduced-motion status.

options.text: Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 3: longer bouncier animation always makes an interface more engaging. Treat the timeline and rendering measurement appearance as sufficient proof.

Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 3: choose every timeline control from feedback purpose and user control, then verify rapid input, interruption, completion, and attention cost. Apply that contract to timeline and rendering measurement and retain changed-case evidence.

Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 3: use animation delay to postpone the actual form submission until the illustration completes. Skip a computed-style trace, animation-frame measurement, paused and interrupted states, and an equivalent reduced-motion status.

options.text: Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 5: a very fast animation automatically satisfies reduced-motion needs. Treat the timeline and rendering measurement appearance as sufficient proof.

Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 5: remove nonessential spatial movement while preserving status and task feedback through immediate nonmotion cues and user control. Apply that contract to timeline and rendering measurement and retain changed-case evidence.

Independent keyframe composition performance and preference build case 5: run the full orbit in one millisecond so it technically spends less time moving. Skip a computed-style trace, animation-frame measurement, paused and interrupted states, and an equivalent reduced-motion status.
